parsed_review.summary: CP00-703 (RP23 External Integrations II finance-integrations P02 review closeout / P03 foundation, risk C, 150 units, RP23.P02.M09.S09-RP23.P03.M06.S12) passes hardened read-only review. The pack binding (registry.js:154) and requirements (registry.js:1134) match the baseline exactly; deliverable and micro-title distributions each sum to 150 and were independently confirmed by counting units in the manifest (implementation 63, contract 31, security_audit 16, failure_recovery 4, etc.). Descriptor/case-set construction (service.js:134-135) is descriptor-only with a correct authority boundary (claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-704). Validators (validators.js:7-8) comprehensively enforce coverage drift, runtime-closed status, no payload/credential/permission/audit leakage, Hermes/Claude gate, and handoff. Tests (model.test.js:34-36) cover CP703 binding/coverage, runtime-closed packets, and the generated contract (latest_pack=CP00-703, validation.valid=true) while preserving CP00-694..702 as historical. Hermes packets emit no runtime receipt and serve only deterministic review/evidence handling; Claude packet is read_only and non-final. The manifest correctly declares production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, descriptor_only=true (no overclaim), and inspection/adjudication/command-evidence are in the correct pending_review pre-review state. No real client/financial/credential data or PII is present in any artifact, and no sensitive-payload flag is set true. Review hardening checks pass: auth preflight succeeded (exit 0, 1436-byte valid JSON), read-tools-only, summary-only review prohibited and not used, forbidden tools absent, and changed-file-scope correctly excludes unrelated dirty files. The pack claims neither runtime nor enterprise trust, so the expected Claude/human authority boundary does not block this CP closeout goal. Provenance note: the 23 validation commands were not re-executed under this read-only review; their reported exit_code:0 evidence was corroborated by direct inspection of the source, validators, tests, and generated contract. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
